title: Review-reports directory creation timing unclear in RULES.md Step 3b
description: RULES.md Step 3b doesn't specify when to create .beads/agent-summaries/{epic-id}/review-reports/ relative to spawning Pantry (review mode). reviews.md says 'before composing review prompts' but RULES.md doesn't mention it. Add explicit directory creation to RULES.md Step 3b.

title: Step 0 wildcard glob may match stale reports from prior review cycles
PII scrub regression: bd sync reverted email scrub in issues.jsonl
AGG-024: Rename Pantry output to eliminate data file term collision
description:
The Pantry writes orchestration artifacts called data files, but RULES.md uses project data files in the Information Diet prohibition. Despite a clarifying note, the collision persists and risks misinterpretation by cold agents.

Fix: Rename Pantry output to prompt files or task briefs and update all references across pantry.md, RULES.md, and implementation.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep for data file in pantry.md returns zero matches (replaced with new term)
- [ ] RULES.md Information Diet section has no terminological overlap with Pantry output naming
- [ ] All files referencing Pantry output use the new consistent term

title: AGG-045: Specify wave parallelism and concurrency rules
description:
README and RULES say prepare next wave while current runs but theres no decision tree for mid-wave failures, early completions, or backfilling. The max 7 Dirt Pushers limit is unjustified. Retry spawn counting against the limit is unclear.

Fix: Add wave management section to RULES.md: justify 7-agent limit, specify retry counting, provide decision tree for failures and backfilling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md contains a Wave Management section with the 7-agent limit justification
- [ ] Retry spawns are explicitly counted (or not) against the concurrent agent limit
- [ ] A decision tree or flowchart covers mid-wave failure, early completion, and backfill scenarios

title: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md bd close ordering creates failure window before summary doc
description:
Root cause: The 'bd close {TASK_ID}' instruction in dirt-pusher-skeleton.md appears as a standalone line after the 6-step numbered list (line 42), structurally disconnected from the step sequence. The instruction is ambiguous about when it executes relative to Step 6 (write summary doc). If an agent closes the bead before writing the summary doc and then crashes, the task is marked closed with no summary artifact. Checkpoint B (checkpoints.md:246) requires reading the summary doc and would find nothing, but the task is already closed.

Affected surfaces:
- dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:42 -- 'After committing: bd close {TASK_ID}' appears orphaned after step list

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 10, P3), Edge Cases (Finding 6, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer rated P2 due to the failure window between bead closure and summary doc creation. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Move 'bd close {TASK_ID}' to an explicit position after Step 6 with a note: 'Close the bead ONLY after the summary doc is successfully written.' Consider making it Step 7.

Acceptance criteria:
- bd close instruction is explicitly sequenced after summary doc write (Step 6)
- Either incorporated into a numbered step or clearly labeled as post-Step-6
- No ambiguity about when bead closure occurs relative to artifact creation

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md shell example uses unquoted SESSION_DIR variable
Handoff Safety
description: Fix silent failures, missing error handling, and underspecified workflows in inter-agent handoff paths

title: big-head.md includes Edit tool unnecessarily, violating least-privilege
Checkpoints epic ID resolution table is overspecified and ambiguous
description: checkpoints.md provides an epic ID resolution table but mixes epic ID format (3-char) with task ID patterns (full format). Does not clearly handle standalone tasks (e.g. hs_website-596y with no .N suffix). Simplify to plain English with annotated examples.

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Tier 2 {session-dir} vs Tier 1 {SESSION_DIR} naming ambiguity
checkpoints.md CCB lists report paths twice (duplication risk)
description:
Root cause: The CCB (Checkpoint C) template in checkpoints.md lists the 4 individual report paths in both the 'Individual reports' section (lines 383-387) and the 'Check 0: Report Existence Verification' section (lines 392-396). The duplication means path format changes must be updated in two places within the same template.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:383-387 -- Individual reports section
- checkpoints.md:392-396 -- Check 0 section (duplicate listing)

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 8, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 maintainability polish.

Fix: In Check 0, reference 'the 4 individual report paths listed above' instead of re-listing them.

Acceptance criteria:
- Report paths appear only once in the CCB template, with Check 0 referencing the earlier listing

title: install-hooks.sh does not ensure sync-to-claude.sh is executable after clone
AGG-019: Add fallback path for TeamCreate unavailability in reviews.md
description:
reviews.md mandates TeamCreate for the Nitpicker review workflow but does not specify what to do if the runtime environment cannot create teams or messaging fails. This is a hard blocker with no graceful degradation.

Fix: Add a fallback path: If TeamCreate is unavailable, spawn reviewers as individual Task agents and coordinate via file-based handoffs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md contains an explicit fallback section for when TeamCreate is unavailable
- [ ] The fallback uses individual Task agents with file-based coordination
- [ ] Both the team path and fallback path produce the same output artifacts (4 review reports)

title: AGG-040: Clarify DMVDC sampling formula with plain English and examples
description:
checkpoints.md requires Pest Control to calculate max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) for sampling review findings. LLMs may miscalculate ceil(), and the formula rationale is unexplained.

Fix: Expand with plain English: minimum 3, maximum 5, otherwise one-third rounded up. Add examples: 7 findings -> 3, 15 findings -> 5.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md DMVDC section includes plain English explanation of the sampling formula
- [ ] At least 3 worked examples show input finding counts and resulting sample sizes
- [ ] The formula and English description produce the same results for all examples

title: sync-to-claude.sh backup timestamp has 1-second collision risk
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Compliance Status section is verbose and redundant with audit table
AGG-023: Standardize path reference conventions
description:
RULES.md uses runtime paths (~/.claude/orchestration/templates/scout.md), README uses shorthand (templates/scout.md), and the File Reference table inconsistently includes or omits orchestration/ prefix. No document declares which namespace each shorthand assumes.

Fix: Add a path convention note declaring: repo paths use orchestration/templates/..., runtime paths use ~/.claude/orchestration/templates/..., shorthand is informal only.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] README and RULES.md contain a path convention declaration
- [ ] The File Reference table uses consistent full repo-root paths
- [ ] RULES.md agent spawn instructions use explicit runtime paths, not shorthand

title: rsync --delete silently removes custom user files from ~/.claude/orchestration/
Remove _standalone references from scout.md metadata templates
description:
scout.md still references _standalone in its per-task metadata format (line 74: 'Epic: {epic-id or _standalone}') and session summary (line 165: 'use _standalone for tasks not in any epic'). The _standalone artifact directory concept was removed in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift).

Fix: Remove _standalone from scout.md metadata templates. Tasks without an epic parent should show 'Epic: none' or omit the field. Keep {epic-id} as a context field since the Queen still passes epic IDs to agents for context.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep for '_standalone' in orchestration/templates/scout.md returns zero matches
- [ ] Tasks without epic parents are handled gracefully (no placeholder value needed)
- [ ] {epic-id} remains available as context metadata for tasks that do have an epic parent
close_reason: Replaced _standalone with none in scout.md lines 74 and 165. No other active files contained _standalone.

title: install-hooks.sh backup uses fixed filename, losing backup history on re-run
install-hooks.sh generated hook lacks descriptive error on sync script failure
AGG-002: Fix pre-push hook not being versioned or installable
description:
The repo claims agents sync on git push via pre-push hook, but .git/hooks/pre-push is local-only outside version control. No installer script, no documented symlink step, no explanation of .beads/hooks/pre-push vs .git/hooks/pre-push relationship.

Fix: Provide a tracked hook installer script (or documented symlink step) and clarify the hook file relationships.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A tracked script (e.g., scripts/install-hooks.sh) exists that installs the pre-push hook
- [ ] Running the installer creates a working .git/hooks/pre-push that triggers sync
- [ ] README or SETUP.md documents the hook installation step with exact commands

title: README fork instructions step 4 lacks actual hook install command
AGG-043: Fix stale and inconsistent README file reference table
description:
The README File Reference table has issues: queen-state.md described as schema but is a template, Read by column uses inconsistent casing, path prefixes mix conventions, and the table doesnt match RULES.md read-ownership rules.

Fix: Audit all entries for accuracy, standardize casing, ensure consistent path prefixes from repo root.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Every entry in the File Reference table matches the actual file contents and purpose
- [ ] Read by column uses consistent article/casing pattern throughout
- [ ] All paths use full repo-root relative paths (orchestration/templates/..., not templates/...)

title: AGG-027: Standardize review type naming between display titles and short names
description:
reviews.md uses Correctness Redux as a display title while filenames and skeleton placeholders use correctness. This inconsistency increases parsing friction in chained prompts.

Fix: Standardize on either the display title or short name consistently in templates and filenames. Document the mapping if both forms are needed.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Each review type has one canonical name used in both templates and filenames
- [ ] If display and short forms both exist, a mapping table documents the correspondence
- [ ] No template uses a review type name that differs from the canonical form without explanation

title: pantry.md Review Mode does not generate Big Head preview file for CCO audit
description:
Root cause: pantry.md Review Mode Step 5 creates preview files for the 4 Nitpicker review types by combining nitpicker-skeleton.md with each data file. However, no preview is generated for Big Head's consolidation prompt. If CCO is meant to audit all prompts before team creation, the Big Head prompt is excluded from preview-based auditing.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:137-146 -- Step 5 (Write Combined Review Previews) omits Big Head

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 9, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 completeness item.

Fix: Either (a) add a Step 5b to create a Big Head preview, or (b) explicitly document that Big Head's prompt is excluded from CCO preview audit and explain why.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either a Big Head preview is generated, or the exclusion is explicitly documented with rationale

title: AGG-006: Create installation and safety documentation for hook/sync workflow
description:
No single document explains how to install the pre-push hook, what the sync script overwrites, how to back up ~/.claude/ content, or how to uninstall the orchestration wiring. Information is scattered across SETUP.md, the sync script, and the hook file.

Fix: Create a dedicated installation guide covering hook installation, sync behavior, backup strategy, and uninstall steps.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A dedicated installation guide document exists with all four sections (install, sync, backup, uninstall)
- [ ] The guide includes exact commands for each step that work on a fresh clone
- [ ] SETUP.md cross-references the new guide instead of duplicating scattered instructions

title: AGG-039: Add complete error recovery procedures
description:
The retry limits table omits Pantry and Scout entirely. No retry path exists for Pantry CCO failures. Stuck-agent escalation lacks diagnostic steps. No wave-level failure threshold exists.

Fix: Add Pantry/Scout to retry table (1 retry, then escalate). Add stuck-agent diagnostic procedure. Add wave failure threshold: if >50% fail, pause and present batch summary to user.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md retry limits table includes entries for Pantry and Scout with retry counts
- [ ] A step-by-step stuck-agent diagnostic procedure is documented (check summary doc, git log, etc.)
- [ ] A wave-level failure threshold (e.g., >50%) triggers pause and user notification

title: SESSION_DIR variable not quoted in shell mkdir command in RULES.md
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 115 uses mkdir -p ${SESSION_DIR}/{task-metadata,previews,prompts} without quoting ${SESSION_DIR}. While the value is a hash (no spaces), shell best practice is to quote variable expansions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- unquoted ${SESSION_DIR} in mkdir command

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 best-practice polish item.

Fix: Change to mkdir -p "${SESSION_DIR}"/{task-metadata,previews,prompts}

Acceptance criteria:
- SESSION_DIR is quoted in the mkdir command

title: AGG-008: Rewrite scout-organizer.md to match scout.md workflow
description:
The Scout agent definition contains a generic agent organizer system prompt referencing unrelated concepts (context managers, JSON payloads, unverifiable metrics). It never mentions the bd CLI workflow, required artifact outputs, or file formats from scout.md. It lists Edit in tools despite never needing it. Cold agents will ignore scout.md instructions.

Fix: Rewrite agents/scout-organizer.md to reference the bd workflow, required artifacts (briefing.md, task-metadata files), and exact formats. Remove Edit from tools, remove unverifiable metrics, remove JSON protocol.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] agents/scout-organizer.md references bd CLI commands and scout.md artifact outputs explicitly
- [ ] Edit is not listed in the tools section of the agent definition
- [ ] No unverifiable performance metrics (e.g., accuracy >95%) appear in the file
close_reason: Rewrote agents/scout-organizer.md: removed Edit from tools, eliminated all unverifiable metrics and JSON protocol, replaced generic boilerplate with bd CLI workflow references and scout.md artifact formats

title: Pantry 'report to Queen immediately' misleading for Task subagent model
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md audit claims All Files Pass despite partial nitpicker-skeleton.md
Queen information diet wording ambiguous about agent data files
description: RULES.md Information Diet says 'DO NOT READ: source code, tests, data files, configs...' but dirt-pusher data files are also 'data files'. A fresh Queen might think she cannot read Pantry verdict tables or preview paths. Clarify: the prohibition is on project data files, not orchestration artifacts like data files and verdict tables.

title: Agent Definitions
description: Fix agent definition files that have generic prompts or missing guidance, causing cold agents to fail their intended workflows

title: AGG-046: Specify queen-state.md recovery and consistency rules
description:
queen-state.md uses an unspecified timestamp format. The Pest Control table has 6 rows but doesnt match RULES.md step count. No source-of-truth precedence is defined when queen-state.md conflicts with git log or artifact files.

Fix: Specify timestamp format matching canonical format, align table rows with RULES.md steps, add Source of Truth precedence note.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] queen-state.md specifies its timestamp format matching the canonical project format
- [ ] The Pest Control table rows align 1:1 with RULES.md step numbers
- [ ] A Source of Truth section defines precedence when queen-state.md conflicts with other state

title: Verification & Checkpoints
description: Define precise thresholds, clarify pipeline boundaries, and add complete error recovery procedures for checkpoint execution

title: Cross-File Contracts
description: Resolve contradictions, conflicts, and undefined authority chains across orchestration template files

title: AGG-018: Align landing instructions between CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
description:
CLAUDE.md includes a Review-findings gate (Step 3) and specific cleanup commands. AGENTS.md omits the review gate and uses generic cleanup. An agent following AGENTS.md could skip the mandatory review gate.

Fix: Add the review gate to AGENTS.md or cross-reference CLAUDE.md as the authoritative landing procedure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md reference the same landing procedure steps
- [ ] The review-findings gate is present or cross-referenced in both files
- [ ] diff of landing sections between files shows no contradictions in step sequence

title: AGG-009: Add severity conflict handling guidance to big-head.md
description:
agents/big-head.md states Priority is the highest across reviewers for merged findings but does not address what to do when reviewer severities differ by 2+ levels. Big Head may silently take the higher severity without flagging calibration disagreement.

Fix: Add guidance: If severity differs by 2+ levels, note the discrepancy in the consolidation log, use the higher severity, and flag it for Queen review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] agents/big-head.md contains explicit guidance for handling 2+ level severity disagreements
- [ ] The guidance specifies: log discrepancy, use higher severity, flag for Queen review
- [ ] A worked example shows the expected behavior (e.g., one reviewer P1, another P3)

title: RULES.md pantry.md section references not explicit
description: pantry-impl.md references 'pantry.md, Section 1' and pantry-review.md references 'pantry.md, Section 2', but RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b just say '→ templates/pantry.md' without section numbers. Add section references to RULES.md for traceability.

title: AGG-010: Resolve timestamp ownership conflict between Queen and Pantry
description:
Three files give contradictory instructions about who generates the review timestamp. pantry.md says use the Queens timestamp, pantry-review.md says generate one, reviews.md says the Queen generates it, but RULES.md Step 3b does not instruct the Queen to do so.

Fix: Pick a single timestamp owner, update all affected files (pantry.md, pantry-review.md, reviews.md, RULES.md), and add the generation step to the owners workflow.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Exactly one file contains the timestamp generation instruction; all others reference it
- [ ] grep for timestamp generation across pantry.md, pantry-review.md, reviews.md, RULES.md shows consistent ownership
- [ ] The chosen owners workflow step explicitly includes when and how to generate the timestamp

title: AGG-029: Add inline acronym expansions to architecture diagram
description:
The README architecture diagram uses CCO, WWD, DMVDC, and CCB without inline expansions. Full names appear much later in the document. First-time readers encounter unexpanded acronyms with no context.

Fix: Add one-line expansions immediately under the diagram (e.g., CCO = Colony Cartography Office, WWD = Wandering Worker Detection, etc.).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] The README architecture diagram section includes inline acronym expansions
- [ ] All four acronyms (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are expanded within 5 lines of first use
- [ ] Expansions appear before the detailed description sections later in the document

title: AGG-005: Fix setup verification test that contradicts Information Diet
description:
The Setup Test it checklist instructs the user to verify Claude runs bd show, but RULES.md prohibits the Queen from running bd show or any bd query commands. Only the Scout should run these.

Fix: Update the verification test to confirm the Scout (not the Queen) runs bd queries.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] SETUP.md test checklist no longer instructs the Queen to run bd show/ready/list
- [ ] Verification step tests that the Scout correctly executes bd queries
- [ ] No contradiction between SETUP.md test steps and RULES.md Information Diet rules

title: AGG-026: Standardize agent name casing and article usage
description:
Agent names are capitalized and articled inconsistently: the Queen / Queen / The Queen, the Nitpickers / Nitpicker team, Big Head always title case. Filenames use kebab-case while prose mixes forms.

Fix: Standardize: lowercase article in prose (the Queen, the Scout), title case for role names, kebab-case in filenames. Document convention in glossary.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] All prose references use consistent article/casing pattern (e.g., the Queen not The Queen)
- [ ] All filenames use kebab-case for agent names
- [ ] The naming convention is documented in the glossary

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md has duplicate {EPIC_ID} entry in Tier 1 examples
close_reason: Fixed by session-scoping commits dabb764/f838cef. EPIC_ID placeholder and multi-epic checkpoint naming were removed entirely.

title: AGG-047: Consolidate review directory creation to single canonical source
description:
The instruction to create review-reports/ directories appears in three places: RULES.md Step 3b, reviews.md Pre-Spawn Directory Setup, and checkpoints.md references. Repetition creates drift risk if one file updates without others.

Fix: Designate RULES.md as canonical source for directory creation, replace duplicates in reviews.md and checkpoints.md with cross-references.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md is the single source for review directory creation instructions
- [ ] reviews.md references RULES.md instead of duplicating directory creation steps
- [ ] checkpoints.md references RULES.md instead of duplicating directory creation steps

title: big-head.md agent definition lacks cross-reference to reviews.md protocol
AGG-041: Clarify Agent type: code-reviewer role in checkpoints.md
description:
Each checkpoint section says Agent type: code-reviewer but checkpoints are executed by Pest Control. A cold Pest Control agent cant tell whether it should BE code-reviewer or SPAWN code-reviewer subagents.

Fix: Clarify: Pest Control runs each checkpoint using code-reviewer agent type for spawned subagent. Pest Control is the orchestrator, not the executor.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md explicitly states Pest Control orchestrates and code-reviewer executes
- [ ] The Agent type field is annotated with spawned agent type to prevent misinterpretation
- [ ] The Pest Control vs code-reviewer distinction is stated once at the top and referenced throughout

title: RULES.md retry counter interaction between per-checkpoint and session-total limits is unspecified
description:
Root cause: The retry limits table in RULES.md (lines 150-151) says Checkpoint C failures allow 1 retry, and a session total of 5 retries. But it does not specify whether a Checkpoint C re-run (which may spawn a new Big Head instance) counts toward the session total. The interaction between per-checkpoint limits and the global session cap is ambiguous.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:150-151 -- Retry limits table, Checkpoint C row

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is an edge case in retry accounting, not a functional failure.

Fix: Clarify in RULES.md retry table: 'Each Checkpoint C re-run counts as 1 toward both the per-checkpoint limit (1) and the session total (5).'

Acceptance criteria:
- Retry table explicitly states how per-checkpoint retries interact with the session total

title: Pantry told to read implementation.md but no explanation of what to extract
description: pantry.md Step 1 says 'Read this file: implementation.md' but gives no guidance on what data to extract or how it shapes the Pantry's output. A fresh Pantry agent would read it and not know what to do with it. Clarify: is it informational (absorb the policy) or extractive (pull specific data)?

title: Multi-epic checkpoint artifact naming undefined
description: checkpoints.md says 'Use multi for consolidations spanning multiple epics' but RULES.md doesn't mention multi-epic scenarios. A fresh Pest Control wouldn't know when to use 'multi' vs individual epic IDs. Clarify whether multi-epic reviews are supported and document the naming convention.
close_reason: Fixed by session-scoping commits dabb764/f838cef. EPIC_ID placeholder and multi-epic checkpoint naming were removed entirely.

title: Dirt-pusher-skeleton uses {TASK_ID} placeholder but doesn't define it
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md defines {TASK_TYPE}, {AGENT_TYPE}, {DATA_FILE_PATH} in its placeholder list but uses {TASK_ID} in the commit message template without defining it. A fresh Queen filling in the skeleton would not know what value to substitute. Add {TASK_ID} to the placeholder definitions.

title: Explore tmux-based agent spawning for real-time observability
description: Explore running orchestrated subagents in tmux windows instead of background Task agents, so you can observe agent work in real-time. Key research questions: (1) Can the boss-bot launch claude CLI sessions in tmux windows via Bash? (2) How to pipe prompts into those sessions? (3) How to collect results back? (4) Hook-based automation options? Goal: Each spawned agent gets its own tmux window you can switch to with ctrl+b n/p. May influence orchestration templates if feasible.

title: Angle-bracket placeholder convention not documented in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md
SETUP.md references hardcoded ~/projects/hs_website/ path unusable by adopters
Scout briefing format lacks optional Errors section placement
Review timestamp ownership contradicts between pantry.md and checkpoints.md
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 2 says the Pantry generates the review timestamp. checkpoints.md says the Queen generates it. A fresh Pantry and a fresh Pest Control would disagree on who owns this value. Pick one owner, document it, and update both files.

title: Review type names inconsistent between reviews.md and file naming
description: reviews.md section titles use 'Correctness Redux', 'Edge Cases (P2)' etc. but file names and checkpoints use 'correctness', 'edge-cases'. Drop 'Redux' and priority annotations from section titles to match the canonical short names used in file naming.

title: Terminology & Naming
description: Define extraction rules, standardize placeholder conventions, variable names, and terminology across all templates

title: Documentation Gaps
description: Add missing documentation: archive warnings, contribution guide, wave management rules, and state recovery procedures

title: AGG-020: Define TASK_SUFFIX extraction rules
description:
Multiple files reference {TASK_SUFFIX} but never define the extraction algorithm. Examples show transformations (e.g., hs_website-74g.1 -> 74g1) but the rule is unstated.

Fix: Add explicit TASK_SUFFIX extraction rules with examples covering all ID patterns in a single canonical location. Cross-reference from scout.md, pantry.md, and checkpoints.md.

Note: {EPIC_ID} was removed from all active templates in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift). Only TASK_SUFFIX extraction needs defining.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A single canonical file defines TASK_SUFFIX extraction with regex or step-by-step rules
- [ ] Examples cover all patterns: dotted (ant-farm-74g.1 -> 74g1), undotted (ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa)
- [ ] scout.md, pantry.md, and checkpoints.md cross-reference the canonical definition instead of local definitions

title: AGG-007: Add quick-start example to README
description:
README.md explains architecture in depth but never provides a minimal do these 3 things path for new adopters. The value proposition is buried and the first actionable step is in a separate doc.

Fix: Add a Quick Start subsection showing the minimal path from clone to first orchestrated session.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] README.md contains a Quick Start section within the first 50 lines
- [ ] The section includes numbered steps (3-5) from clone to first orchestrated run
- [ ] At least one concrete input/output example shows what the user types and what they see

title: Checkpoint A.5 relies on Queen-provided file list with no independent scope validation
description:
Root cause: Checkpoint A.5 (checkpoints.md:189) uses {list files from task description} as the expected file list, provided by the Queen. If the Queen passes an incomplete or incorrect list, A.5 will produce false positives or false negatives for scope violations. The checkpoint has no independent way to derive expected scope.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:189 -- Checkpoint A.5 expected file list placeholder

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 4, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is a known limitation of the input-passing design, not a bug.

Fix: Either have Pest Control cross-reference against the original data file at a known path, or document this as a known limitation: 'A.5 accuracy depends on the Queen file list -- review WARN verdicts carefully.'

Acceptance criteria:
- Either A.5 has an independent scope reference, or the limitation is explicitly documented

title: RULES.md Queen Prohibitions still says 'data files' instead of 'project data files' (ant-farm-6jv gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit c3771df (ant-farm-6jv) disambiguated 'data files' in the Information Diet section (line 80) to 'project data files' with an explanatory note. However, the Queen Prohibitions section at RULES.md line 6 still reads 'data files' without the 'project' qualifier. A Queen reading the Prohibitions section first (as instructed) encounters the ambiguity that ant-farm-6jv was meant to fix, before reaching the clarification 74 lines later.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:6 -- 'NEVER read source code, tests, data files, or config files' should say 'project data files'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 3, P2), Excellence (Finding 4, P3)
Taking highest severity: P2. Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates ant-farm-6jv acceptance criteria.

Fix: Change 'data files' to 'project data files' on line 6 of RULES.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- RULES.md line 6 reads 'project data files' instead of 'data files'
- Consistent with the Information Diet section disambiguation

title: big-head-skeleton.md wiring Step 3 assumes Queen can SendMessage to team members
description:
Root cause: The wiring instructions in big-head-skeleton.md Step 3 (lines 32-43) tell the Queen to use SendMessage(to='big-head', message=...) after the 4 Nitpickers finish. However, in Claude Code's team model, the Queen (team lead/parent) may not be able to send messages to team members via SendMessage. The fallback on lines 42-43 -- 'Big Head can also discover them from the brief if SendMessage is delayed' -- partially mitigates this, but the primary mechanism documented in Step 3 may not function as described.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:32-43 -- wiring Step 3 SendMessage instruction

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but architectural in nature. P2 because if the primary communication mechanism does not work, Big Head relies entirely on the fallback path.

Fix: Clarify the actual communication mechanism. Options: (a) make the data file the primary mechanism and remove or demote the SendMessage step, (b) have a Nitpicker reviewer forward paths to Big Head after completion, (c) document that the brief file IS the primary mechanism and SendMessage is supplementary. Whichever approach is chosen, document it as canonical.

Acceptance criteria:
- The primary mechanism for Big Head to receive report paths is clearly documented
- No reliance on a communication pattern that may not be supported by Claude Code's team model
- The fallback path (brief-based discovery) is either promoted to primary or explicitly documented as the backup

title: AGG-013: Resolve user interaction contradiction between reviews.md and RULES.md
description:
reviews.md instructs asking the user about fixing review findings, re-introducing a synchronous blocking step that contradicts RULES.md design of minimizing user interaction to a single approval at Step 1.

Fix: Decide whether post-review user triage is mandatory. If yes, add it to RULES.md with skip conditions. If no, remove the interactive prompt from reviews.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md and RULES.md agree on whether post-review user triage is a required step
- [ ] If the step exists, RULES.md documents it with explicit skip conditions
- [ ] No undocumented synchronous blocking steps exist outside RULES.md step sequence

title: Scout ready mode gaps: Step 4 references bd blocked not run, assumption undocumented
Timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS repeated 5+ times across files
description: The timestamp format is redefined in checkpoints.md at lines 31, 97, 152, 277, 443 and in pantry.md line 101. If the format ever changes, all 5+ locations must be updated. Define once at the top of checkpoints.md and reference it elsewhere.

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md audit table line numbers will become stale without commit reference
Setup & Forkability
description: Fix sync script, hook installation, PII exposure, and documentation issues blocking adoption and forkability

title: Add glossary to RULES.md defining bead, epic, wave, dirt pusher
description: Terms like 'bead', 'epic', 'wave', 'dirt pusher', 'nitpicker' are used throughout orchestration files but never defined. A fresh agent must infer meaning from context. Add a Terminology section to RULES.md with concise definitions.

title: AGG-035: Add remediation path for missing Nitpicker reports
description:
reviews.md instructs Big Head not to proceed until all 4 reports are present but never says what to DO about a missing report. No messaging, no error return, no timeout specified.

Fix: Add remediation: Return an error to the Queen listing missing reports and requesting re-spawn. Do NOT wait indefinitely.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md Big Head section includes a remediation step for missing reports
- [ ] The step specifies: return error to Queen, list missing reports, request re-spawn
- [ ] A timeout or maximum wait is specified before triggering the remediation path

title: Epic artifact directory creation not enforced in RULES.md
description: RULES.md says the Queen creates epic directories at Step 2 but provides no explicit timing relative to Pantry spawn. reviews.md separately says to create review-reports/ before composing review prompts. A fresh Queen must manually create directories at different times with no consolidated checklist. Add a pre-spawn directory setup block to RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b.

title: AGG-032: Add fallback for missing or malformed Scout metadata in Pantry
description:
The Pantry extracts data from Scout metadata files and is forbidden from running bd show to fill gaps. If Scout wrote incomplete metadata, the Pantry has no fallback. Placeholder validation syntax also risks false positives.

Fix: Add fail-fast instructions: If metadata is incomplete or contains placeholder text, FAIL immediately with error details. Distinguish instruction syntax from surviving-placeholder syntax.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] pantry.md contains fail-fast instructions for incomplete or placeholder-contaminated metadata
- [ ] The placeholder detection syntax is distinct from template instruction text (no false positives)
- [ ] A partial verdict table is returned when metadata fails, not a silent skip

title: AGG-044: Create CONTRIBUTING guide for extending the framework
description:
Adopters who want to add agents, modify templates, or change checkpoints have no guidance on safe extension without breaking cross-file contracts. No docs for adding checkpoints, modifying templates, testing changes, or cross-file dependencies.

Fix: Create CONTRIBUTING.md covering: Adding a new checkpoint, Modifying agent templates, Testing changes, and Syncing to ~/.claude/.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md exists with sections for adding checkpoints, modifying templates, testing, and syncing
- [ ] Each section lists the cross-file dependencies that must be updated together
- [ ] A testing procedure describes how to validate changes before deploying to ~/.claude/

title: Session directory passing mechanism not shown in RULES.md
description: RULES.md Step 0 generates SESSION_ID and creates the session dir. Scout.md expects {SESSION_DIR} as input. But RULES.md doesn't show HOW the Queen passes the session dir path to the Scout or any other agent. Add explicit instruction: pass session dir as a parameter in the spawn prompt.

title: big-head-skeleton.md: CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH missing leading dot in .beads/ path
description:
Root cause: Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md defines {CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH} as 'beads/agent-summaries/...' instead of '.beads/agent-summaries/...'. The missing leading dot means the Queen would substitute a path that writes the consolidated report to a non-existent 'beads/' directory at the repo root instead of the canonical '.beads/' hidden directory.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- placeholder definition uses 'beads/' instead of '.beads/'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 1, P2), Excellence (Finding 1, P2)
Both reviewers independently identified this and cross-referenced each other. Confirmed as a genuine path error that would cause wrong-directory writes at fill-time.

Fix: Change 'beads/agent-summaries/' to '.beads/agent-summaries/' on line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md starts with '.beads/agent-summaries/'
- The path matches the convention used in all other orchestration files

title: Placeholder syntax inconsistent across orchestration files
description: Orchestration files mix three placeholder syntaxes: {curly-lowercase} for substitution, {UPPERCASE} for substitution, and <angle-brackets> for substitution. A fresh agent cannot tell which are literal vs to-be-substituted. Standardize: {UPPERCASE} = substitute, document convention at top of each file that uses placeholders.

title: AGG-012: Resolve agent-type authority conflict between skeleton and Pantry
description:
dirt-pusher-skeleton.md says The Queen MAY override the agent type, while pantry.md says Do NOT re-evaluate or override the Scout recommendation. This creates an unresolved authority branch.

Fix: Decide the authority chain explicitly (Scout recommends, Pantry passes through, Queen may override with documented rationale) and update both files.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md and pantry.md state the same authority chain for agent-type selection
- [ ] The override policy is documented in exactly one place with cross-references from both files
- [ ] If Queen override is allowed, the conditions and documentation requirements are specified

title: Big Head data file structure not documented in pantry.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain but doesn't show the exact markdown format. The structure lives in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol. A fresh Pantry might produce the wrong format. Either inline the format or add an explicit cross-reference.

title: Scout {MODE} placeholder parsing convention undocumented for compound modes
Scout Step 2.5 silently skips agent files with invalid frontmatter
AGG-003: Scrub personal identifiers from .beads/issues.jsonl
description:
The tracked issues database contains owner:"correycc@gmail.com" on every record. For a public release, this is a privacy hazard and forkability blocker since adopters inherit another users identity.

Fix: Scrub PII from the issues file, document it as sample data to be reset on fork, or gitignore it and provide a bd init step.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep for email addresses in .beads/issues.jsonl returns zero matches
- [ ] New adopters running bd init get a clean issues database without inherited identities
- [ ] README documents the fork/init step for new adopters
close_reason: Scrubbed correycc@gmail.com from all 100 owner fields and 1 description field in .beads/issues.jsonl. Added 'Forking this repo' section to README.md with bd init --prefix steps. Commit 047f1be.

title: Pest Control CCO review section doesn't specify where commit range comes from
description: checkpoints.md CCO section for review prompts says 'Run git diff --name-only <first-commit>..<last-commit>' but doesn't explain how Pest Control receives these values. A fresh Pest Control agent would waste time looking for the source. Add: 'The Queen provides the commit range in the spawn prompt.'

title: Task ID variable naming inconsistent across templates
description: Some files use {task-id-suffix}, some use {task-id}, some use {TASK_ID}. These may refer to the same value or different values. A fresh agent cannot tell. Standardize variable names: pick one convention and find-replace across all templates.

title: checkpoints.md dangling cross-reference to non-existent 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' section
description:
Root cause: Three locations in checkpoints.md reference a section titled 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' that does not exist in the file. The closest match is 'Pest Control Overview' (line 9). The dangling cross-references appear at lines 67, 243, and 300.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:67 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint A prompt
- checkpoints.md:243 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint B prompt
- checkpoints.md:300 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint B prompt

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 7, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 as this is a documentation inconsistency that could confuse Pest Control but does not break functionality.

Fix: Either (a) rename 'Pest Control Overview' section to 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' to match the references, or (b) update the three references to say 'Pest Control Overview'.

Acceptance criteria:
- The section name referenced at lines 67, 243, and 300 matches an actual section heading in checkpoints.md

title: Read Confirmation finding counts can be copied rather than independently verified
AGG-036: Define checkpoint verdict thresholds and tie-breaking rules
description:
Multiple checkpoint verdict criteria lack precise thresholds. CCO WARN uses small file without defining it. DMVDC says pick 2 most critical criteria without tie-breaking guidance. WWD WARN doesnt clarify whether it blocks the queue.

Fix: Define: small file = <100 lines, tie-breaking = first 2 listed, WARN = soft PASS (continue to DMVDC but flag for Queen review).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md defines small file with a concrete line count threshold
- [ ] DMVDC section includes tie-breaking rule for equally critical criteria
- [ ] WWD section defines WARN behavior explicitly (blocks vs continues with flag)

title: AGG-025: Create canonical glossary for key terms
description:
Key operational terms lack canonical definitions. Wave appears without defining boundaries. CCO/WWD/DMVDC/CCB are expanded differently across files. Ant metaphor names are never mapped to role descriptions in a single table.

Fix: Add a Glossary section to README.md or create orchestration/GLOSSARY.md with canonical definitions for all terms, acronyms, and metaphor mappings.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A glossary document exists with definitions for all framework terms (wave, checkpoint, etc.)
- [ ] All checkpoint acronyms (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are expanded with one-line descriptions
- [ ] All ant metaphor names (Queen, Scout, Pantry, Dirt Pusher, etc.) map to role descriptions

title: big-head-skeleton.md not updated during terminology standardization (ant-farm-ss6 gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit 03f6299 (ant-farm-ss6) standardized task ID terminology across orchestration templates, adding canonical term definitions blocks and normalizing placeholder naming. big-head-skeleton.md was missed. Three separate issues stem from this single omission:

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses {epic-id} (lowercase/hyphenated) instead of {EPIC_ID} (uppercase/underscored)
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses <timestamp> (angle brackets) instead of {timestamp} (curly braces)
- big-head-skeleton.md:1-47 -- missing the canonical term definitions block present in pantry.md:5-8, checkpoints.md:4-7, dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:8-11

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Findings 1,2,3 -- P3), Correctness (Finding 2 -- P2), Excellence (Findings 2,3 -- P3)
Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates the acceptance criteria of ant-farm-ss6. Taking the highest severity: P2.

Fix: (1) Add canonical term definitions block after line 6 of big-head-skeleton.md. (2) Change {epic-id} to {EPIC_ID} on line 14. (3) Change <timestamp> to {timestamp} on line 14.

Acceptance criteria:
- big-head-skeleton.md contains the same term definitions block as pantry.md, checkpoints.md, and dirt-pusher-skeleton.md
- All placeholders use {UPPERCASE} naming convention
- All timestamp references use {timestamp} (curly braces)

title: MANDATORY keyword formatting inconsistent across templates
description: implementation.md uses plain 'MANDATORY', dirt-pusher-skeleton uses 'MANDATORY keyword present', checkpoints uses '(MANDATORY GATE)'. No consistent formatting. Pick one style (e.g. **MANDATORY**) and standardize.

title: SETUP.md test checklist says Queen runs bd show, contradicts Information Diet
Scout metadata write-each-immediately has no truncation/atomicity check downstream
Scout error metadata template lacks context fields (Title, Epic) present in success template
Epic ID vs epic suffix terminology mixed across files
description: Some places say 'epic ID' (e.g. RULES.md), some say 'epic suffix' (e.g. checkpoints.md line 29: 'Use 3-char epic suffix'). These refer to the same value (e.g. 74g). Pick one term and standardize.

title: Find a better name for Pantry 'data files'
description: The Pantry template references 'data files' — find a more descriptive/thematic name that fits the ant-farm metaphor.

title: prompts/ directory creation is redundant between RULES.md Step 0 and pantry.md Review Mode
description:
Root cause: RULES.md Step 0 (line 86/115) creates the prompts/ directory via brace expansion in mkdir. pantry.md Review Mode Step 3 (line 107/110) also says 'Create the prompts directory if needed.' The redundancy is harmless (mkdir -p is idempotent) but creates confusion about who owns directory creation.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- mkdir -p includes prompts/ via brace expansion
- pantry.md:107-110 -- 'Create the prompts directory if needed'

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 11, P3), Correctness (Finding 7, P3)
Both reviewers independently identified the same redundancy. P3 because the behavior is correct either way.

Fix: Keep both (belt-and-suspenders) but add a comment in pantry.md: 'The Queen pre-creates this directory at Step 0, but create if needed as a safety net.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The intentional redundancy is documented with a comment in pantry.md

title: Pantry review return format doesn't show Big Head consolidation file
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 6 return table shows 4 review types but the Big Head consolidation data file path is listed separately below the table. Big-head-skeleton.md Step 0 says 'Read your consolidation brief from {DATA_FILE_PATH}'. A fresh Queen might miss the consolidation file path when parsing Pantry output. Make it prominent.

title: AGG-030: Fix silent task dropping when Scout bd show fails
description:
scout.md instructs the Scout to skip a tasks metadata file if bd show fails. But the Pantry expects metadata for every task ID the Queen passes. If metadata is missing, the task is silently dropped without the Queen knowing.

Fix: Scout writes a placeholder metadata file with error details instead of skipping. Pantry fails the individual task with clear error message if metadata is missing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md instructs writing placeholder error metadata instead of skipping failed tasks
- [ ] pantry.md includes fail-fast logic for missing or error-placeholder metadata files
- [ ] A failed bd show produces a metadata file that the Pantry can detect and report to the Queen

title: AGG-016: Fix contradictory Big Head model assignment
description:
reviews.md specifies Big Head: opus as the model, but the skeleton template has no model specification. A Queen following both instructions has no mechanism to set the Big Head model to opus in the TeamCreate call.

Fix: Add model configuration instructions to TeamCreate call format or move model specification into the skeleton template.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] The Big Head model (opus) is specified in exactly one authoritative location
- [ ] The TeamCreate call format or skeleton template includes the model parameter
- [ ] A cold Queen can determine the correct model for Big Head without ambiguity

title: Big Head spawning mechanics unclear for fresh Queen
description: big-head-skeleton.md says Big Head is a team member (NOT a separate Task agent) but uses {PLACEHOLDER} syntax from Task tool templates. A fresh Queen would not know how to wire this through TeamCreate + SendMessage. Add explicit Queen-facing instructions showing the TeamCreate → SendMessage workflow with an example.

title: Correctness Redux instructs Nitpickers to run bd show with undocumented tool dependency
big-head.md bd create has no error handling for CLI failures mid-consolidation
AGG-021: Define and standardize placeholder conventions
description:
scout.md uses both {SESSION_DIR} (uppercase) and {session-dir} (lowercase, hyphenated) without defining the convention. No file defines whether uppercase means Queen-substituted before spawn vs lowercase means agent-derived at runtime. Cold agents cannot distinguish which to substitute.

Fix: Standardize a convention (e.g., {UPPERCASE} = Queen-substituted, {lowercase} = agent-derived), document in one canonical location, apply across all templates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A canonical document defines the placeholder convention with clear uppercase vs lowercase rules
- [ ] All templates use consistent casing matching the documented convention
- [ ] grep across orchestration/ for placeholder patterns shows no violations of the convention
close_reason: Created orchestration/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md with 3-tier placeholder convention. All templates audited and compliant. Commit 1f656e7.

title: AGG-038: Clarify wildcard artifact path matching in reviews.md transition gate
description:
reviews.md specifies verifying artifacts with wildcard * for the timestamp portion. Multiple files could match due to retries, and the instruction doesnt specify which to check.

Fix: Clarify: Verify at least one DMVDC artifact exists with PASS verdict. If multiple exist, the most recent by timestamp must show PASS.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md transition gate specifies which artifact to check when multiple match the wildcard
- [ ] The most-recent-by-timestamp rule is documented for retry scenarios
- [ ] The PASS verdict requirement is explicit (not just file existence)

title: AGG-011: Fix contradictory Queen read permissions in RULES.md
description:
RULES.md contradicts itself on what the Queen may read. It says NEVER read agent template files but instructs reading skeleton templates. It forbids bd output but expects briefings containing synthesized bd output. It never instructs reading briefing.md which contains strategy details.

Fix: Create an explicit Queen Read Permissions section in RULES.md listing exactly which files the Queen reads vs delegates. Add Read briefing.md to Step 1.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md contains a Queen Read Permissions section with explicit allowed/forbidden file lists
- [ ] The section distinguishes pre-digested metadata (permitted) from raw bd output (forbidden)
- [ ] Step 1 explicitly instructs the Queen to read {SESSION_DIR}/briefing.md

title: AGG-001: Fix sync script data loss and clean-environment failures
description:
scripts/sync-to-claude.sh silently overwrites ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, uses rsync --delete to remove content from ~/.claude/orchestration/, and fails in clean environments because it assumes target directories exist. The rsync dependency is unlisted.

Fix: Add mkdir -p for all target directories, backup or prompt before overwriting CLAUDE.md, replace --delete with safer default (or require --force flag), list rsync as prerequisite.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] mkdir -p guards added for ~/.claude/agents/, ~/.claude/orchestration/, and any other target dirs
- [ ] Running sync-to-claude.sh on a fresh system (no ~/.claude/) succeeds without errors
- [ ] Existing ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is backed up before overwrite (backup file exists or user prompted)

title: AGG-004: Replace hardcoded author-local paths in SETUP.md
description:
Multiple commands in SETUP.md reference ~/projects/hs_website/, a path specific to the authors workstation. Other commands bake in personal context without marking them as examples. These fail for every adopter.

Fix: Replace all author-local paths with generic examples and mark them as adapt to your environment.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep for hs_website in orchestration/SETUP.md returns zero matches
- [ ] All example paths use generic placeholders like ~/projects/your-project/
- [ ] Each example command block has a note indicating paths should be adapted

title: Pantry fail-fast check uses redundant 'missing, does not exist' phrasing
Read Confirmation table placement and duplication in consolidated summary format
AGG-033: Clarify underspecified scout.md workflow steps
description:
scout.md excludes orchestration agents from Dirt Pusher recommendations without explaining why, risking agents excluding them from the catalog entirely. Strategy proposals dont specify whether strategies can overlap or must be complete alternatives.

Fix: Clarify exclusions: Do not recommend for Dirt Pusher tasks but DO include in catalog. Clarify strategies: Each is a complete alternative where every ready task appears in exactly one.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md explicitly states orchestration agents appear in catalog but not Dirt Pusher recommendations
- [ ] Strategy proposal instructions state each strategy is a complete, non-overlapping alternative
- [ ] Every ready task must appear in exactly one strategy per the documented rule

title: checkpoints.md {checkpoint} placeholder not defined in term definitions block
description:
Root cause: The filename pattern on line 20 of checkpoints.md uses {checkpoint} as a placeholder, but this is not defined in the term definitions block (lines 4-7). While its meaning is inferable from context, it breaks the convention of all placeholders being explicitly defined.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:20 -- {checkpoint} used in filename pattern without definition

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 7, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 consistency polish.

Fix: Add a brief definition: '{checkpoint} is the checkpoint abbreviation: cco, wwd, dmvdc, or ccb'

Acceptance criteria:
- {checkpoint} is defined in the term definitions block or has an explanatory note

title: sync-to-claude.sh agent glob fails silently when agents/ directory missing
AGG-015: Fix git pull rebase invalidating correctness review
description:
implementation.md instructs agents to review every changed file (Step 4), then run git pull --rebase before committing (Step 5). If rebase resolves merge conflicts, the Step 4 correctness review is stale.

Fix: Add to Step 5: After git pull --rebase, if any conflicts were resolved, re-run Step 4 on merged files before committing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] implementation.md Step 5 contains a conditional re-review instruction after rebase
- [ ] The instruction specifies the trigger condition (conflicts resolved during rebase)
- [ ] The re-review scope is limited to files affected by the conflict resolution

title: AGG-017: Remove circular reference in Pantry Big Head data file instructions
description:
pantry.md instructs the Pantry to read reviews.md before composing the Big Head data file, but pantry.md Section 2 already IS the review mode instructions. This creates a circular dependency where Pantry reads pantry.md which says read reviews.md which may reference pantry.md.

Fix: Inline required Big Head information into pantry.md Section 2, or clarify the reference is forward-only (read reviews.md for Big Head output format, not Pantry workflow).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] pantry.md Section 2 is self-contained for Big Head data file composition (no circular refs)
- [ ] If reviews.md is still referenced, the reference specifies exactly which section to read and why
- [ ] A cold Pantry agent can compose the Big Head data file by reading only pantry.md

title: AGG-028: Standardize timestamp format string across templates
description:
checkpoints.md specifies YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS (uppercase) but examples show YYYYMMDD-HHmmss (lowercase minutes). big-head-skeleton.md defines yet another format with UTC qualifier. These could cause mismatched paths or naming errors.

Fix: Standardize to YYYYMMDD-HHmmss with UTC qualifier. Define in one canonical location and reference from all templates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] One canonical location defines the timestamp format string with UTC qualifier
- [ ] grep for timestamp format definitions across orchestration/ shows all match the canonical format
- [ ] All examples in templates use the standardized format string

title: AGG-014: Fix undefined AskUserQuestion tool reference in CLAUDE.md
description:
The prompt-engineering workflow in CLAUDE.md requires AskUserQuestion as a tool name, but no compatibility note exists for environments where the tool is named differently (e.g., AskQuestion in Cursor). Cold agents will fail invoking a nonexistent tool.

Fix: Use platform-correct tool name, add compatibility note for variants, or abstract to ask the user using the available question tool.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md prompt-engineering workflow uses a platform-agnostic tool reference or documents variants
- [ ] The instruction works without modification in Claude Code (where AskUserQuestion exists)
- [ ] A compatibility note lists known tool name variants across platforms

title: Task ID suffix terminology inconsistent across templates
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md uses <task-id>, pantry.md uses {task-id-suffix}, checkpoints.md uses both interchangeably. A fresh agent cannot tell if it should use the full ID (hs_website-74g.1) or just the suffix (74g.1). Standardize to one term across all orchestration files.

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Enforcement Strategy mixes completed and aspirational items
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md not cross-referenced from RULES.md or any template
checkpoints.md Checkpoint A Nitpickers uses 0-based numbering (0-6) while Dirt Pushers uses 1-based (1-7)
description:
Root cause: Within checkpoints.md, the Checkpoint A section for Nitpickers numbers its 7 checks as 0-6, while the Checkpoint A section for Dirt Pushers numbers its 7 checks as 1-7. Both claim '7 checks' (correct count), but the inconsistent indexing style within the same file and same checkpoint type creates confusion and could cause off-by-one reporting errors if Pest Control references checks by number.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:154 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist uses 0-based indexing (0-6)
- (Contrast: Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A at line 96 uses 1-based indexing 1-7)

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but rated P2 due to the potential for off-by-one errors in verification reporting.

Fix: Renumber the Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist from 1-7 to match the Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both Checkpoint A sections (Dirt Pushers and Nitpickers) use 1-based numbering
- Check count text ('7 checks') matches the actual numbered items

title: Session ID collision risk from truncated shasum with epoch-second input
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 85 generates session IDs via 'date +%s | shasum | head -c 6' which produces a 6-char hex string. The input is epoch seconds, so two Queens started within the same second would produce identical session IDs, causing artifact directory collisions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:85 -- Session ID generation command

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 1, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because concurrent Queens in the same repo are already implied to be unsupported by concurrency rules.

Fix: Either add per-invocation randomness (e.g., '(date +%s%N; echo $$) | shasum | head -c 6') or document that concurrent Queens are unsupported.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either the session ID has sufficient entropy to avoid same-second collisions, or the single-Queen constraint is explicitly documented

title: Missing prompts/ directory creation before Pantry writes
description: Pantry writes data files to {session-dir}/prompts/ but nobody creates that directory. RULES.md Step 0 creates {session-dir}/{task-metadata,previews} but prompts/ is not included. A fresh Pantry agent would fail on its first write attempt. Fix: add prompts/ to the mkdir -p command in RULES.md Step 0, or have Pantry create it.

title: Verification Pipeline no defined precedence when Big Head and CCB checks disagree
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md does not document or validate angle-bracket placeholder syntax
AGG-022: Standardize variable naming across templates
description:
The same concepts use different variable names across files: {task-id-suffix} vs {TASK_SUFFIX}, standalone vs _standalone, and task ID vs bead ID. This creates confusion and drift.

Fix: Standardize on canonical variable names ({TASK_ID} for full ID, {TASK_SUFFIX} for suffix, {SESSION_DIR} for session directory). Create a shared glossary and reference from all templates. Remove local redefinitions.

Note: {EPIC_ID} was removed from all active templates in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift). It is no longer a variable to standardize.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] All templates use the same variable name for each concept (no synonyms like task-id-suffix vs TASK_SUFFIX)
- [ ] A glossary section defines each canonical variable name with its meaning
- [ ] grep for deprecated variable names across orchestration/ returns zero matches

title: Checkpoints CCO FAIL verdict format has no example
description: checkpoints.md shows PASS verdict format but no example of a FAIL verdict with specific check failures listed. A fresh Pest Control agent might format FAIL output incorrectly. Add a FAIL example showing check number, name, and evidence.

title: checkpoints.md sampling formula contradicts 'minimum 3' prose text
description:
Root cause: checkpoints.md line 311 specifies the formula min(5, ceil(N/3)) for selecting findings to spot-check, but the same line states 'minimum 3, or all findings if fewer than 3'. The formula can produce values below 3 -- e.g., for N=6, ceil(6/3)=2, then min(5,2)=2. The English text says minimum 3 but the formula does not enforce this floor. Pest Control could either under-sample (following formula) or ignore the formula (following prose), creating verification ambiguity.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:311 -- formula/prose contradiction in Checkpoint B Nitpickers sampling rule

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 8, P3 with cross-ref to edge-cases), Edge Cases (Finding 10, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer confirmed this as a genuine behavioral risk at P2. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Replace min(5, ceil(N/3)) with max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) to enforce the stated minimum of 3. Rewrite the prose in plain English: 'Pick at least 3 findings (or all findings if fewer than 3 exist), up to a maximum of 5.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The formula on line 311 enforces a floor of 3
- The English description matches the formula exactly
- No contradiction between formula and prose

title: Wave concept used in RULES.md but never defined
description: RULES.md Hard Gates table references 'Next agent in wave' and checkpoints.md says 'BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave'. The concept of a wave (batch of parallel agents) is never defined. Add to glossary: 'Wave: a batch of agents spawned in parallel. Wave N completes before Wave N+1 begins.'

title: Verification Pipeline Rationale has redundant 'Why both?' paragraph
AGG-031: Add data file format specification to skeleton templates
description:
Skeleton templates say Read your task context from {DATA_FILE_PATH} but never specify the file is markdown, dont list expected sections, and provide no parsing guidance. A cold agent might expect JSON or YAML.

Fix: Add a brief format note to each skeleton (e.g., The data file is markdown with sections: Context, Scope Boundaries, Focus.).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md specifies the data file format and expected sections
- [ ] nitpicker-skeleton.md specifies the data file format and expected sections
- [ ] Both skeletons explicitly state the file is markdown (not JSON/YAML)

title: AGG-034: Fix circular distinct approaches definition in implementation.md
description:
implementation.md defines distinct approaches as requiring different tradeoff profiles, but genuinely different algorithms can share the same tradeoff profile. The definition is circular and confuses Dirt Pushers during design exploration.

Fix: Simplify to concrete criteria: Distinct means different algorithms, OR different data structures, OR different architectural patterns. Cosmetic variations do not count.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] implementation.md defines distinct approaches using concrete criteria, not tradeoff profiles
- [ ] The definition lists specific categories (algorithms, data structures, architectural patterns)
- [ ] A brief example distinguishes distinct from cosmetic variation

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md validation regex Pattern 4 has false negatives for mixed casing
Big Head data file format split between pantry.md and reviews.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain (4 report paths, dedup protocol, bead filing instructions, consolidated output path) but the actual format/structure is in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. A fresh Pantry would need to cross-reference but there's no explicit pointer. Add cross-reference from pantry.md Step 4 to reviews.md.

title: SETUP.md nested code fences break standard markdown rendering
AGG-042: Add archive exclusion warnings to prevent contamination
description:
orchestration/_archive/ contains older documents that match active template glob patterns. An agent using Glob with orchestration/**/*.md would read stale instructions contradicting current workflows. No DO NOT READ _archive warnings exist.

Fix: Add DO NOT READ files in orchestration/_archive/ to active templates Information Diet section. Consider renaming to _deprecated/.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Information Diet section explicitly excludes orchestration/_archive/ from reads
- [ ] At least one other actively-searched template includes the _archive exclusion warning
- [ ] Agents following Information Diet rules will not read archived files via glob patterns

title: AGG-037: Clarify Big Head verification pipeline boundaries and gaps
description:
Both Big Head (Step 0) and Pest Control CCB (Check 0) verify all 4 Nitpicker reports exist. The redundancy is intentional but undocumented. Big Head also lacks verification that it actually read all reports.

Fix: Clarify: Big Head Step 0 is prerequisite (FAIL before reading), CCB Check 0 is audit (FAIL if skipped). Add read verification: After reading, confirm counts from each report.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md documents Big Head Step 0 as prerequisite and CCB Check 0 as audit with distinct descriptions
- [ ] Big Head output includes read confirmation with finding counts per report
- [ ] The two-layer verification design rationale is documented

title: Templates lack explicit guards for missing or empty input artifacts
description:
Root cause: Multiple templates assume their input artifacts exist and are well-formed without specifying explicit error behavior when they are missing or empty. This is a systematic gap across the template suite -- each template handles the happy path but does not specify what to write or return when a required input is absent.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:26 -- No validation that task-metadata files exist before reading (Step 2)
- pantry.md:26-32 -- No handling for empty or malformed metadata fields (e.g., feature tasks with no 'Root cause')
- pantry.md:94 -- Review mode has no handling for empty changed-file list
- big-head-skeleton.md:23 -- Step 1 says 'FAIL immediately if any missing' but does not specify where to write a failure artifact
- checkpoints.md:259 -- No specification for handling bd show failures in Checkpoint B

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Findings 2, 3, 7, 8, 9 -- all P3)
All five findings share the same pattern: expected input artifact may be missing/empty, and the template does not specify explicit error behavior. P3 because each individual case is unlikely but the pattern is systemic.

Fix: Establish a convention across all templates: 'If an expected input artifact is missing or empty, write a brief failure artifact to the expected output path explaining the issue, then return FAIL with error details.' Apply this pattern to Pantry (missing/malformed metadata, empty file list), Big Head (missing reports), and Checkpoints (bd show failures).

Acceptance criteria:
- Each template has explicit instructions for handling missing/empty inputs
- Failure artifacts are written to expected output paths so downstream consumers are not left guessing
- Infrastructure failures (tool unavailability) are distinguished from substance failures (agent quality)

title: Pantry review mode has no fail-fast for empty changed-file list
